sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Applied Materials Upgrades Link AI Capex Boom To Stretch Valuation Concerns
feed.summary: Multiple major brokerages have upgraded Applied Materials (NasdaqGS:AMAT) due to an anticipated increase in semiconductor capital spending driven by AI. Applied Materials, a key supplier of wafer fabrication equipment, is expected to benefit from increased capital expenditures by chipmakers like TSMC and Intel. While broker upgrades link the company directly to AI-related growth, the stock is currently flagged as trading significantly above its estimated fair value by Simply Wall St.

feed.title: User | thepilotnews.com - PGIM Introduces Two Active Muni ETFs
feed.summary: PGIM has introduced two new actively managed municipal bond ETFs. These ETFs aim to provide investors with opportunities in the municipal bond market, leveraging active management strategies. The launch reflects PGIM's expansion into the exchange-traded fund space for municipal securities.

feed.title: Edison International (NYSE:EIX) Price Target Raised to $66.00
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has raised its price target for Edison International (EIX) to $66.00 from $65.00, reiterating a "neutral" rating. This implies a potential upside of 6.14% from the previous close, with the company's stock currently trading around $62.18. The consensus among analysts remains a "Hold" with a target price of $63.30, considering mixed opinions from various brokerages.

feed.title: Jefferies Financial Group Cuts Edison International (NYSE:EIX) Price Target to $54.00
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has lowered its price target for Edison International (NYSE:EIX) from $59.00 to $54.00, while maintaining a "hold" rating, suggesting a potential downside of 13.16%. This adjustment comes amidst mixed analyst sentiment, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average target price of $63.30. Edison International recently reported stronger-than-expected quarterly earnings and revenue, but the stock currently trades at $62.18 with a market cap of approximately $23.9 billion.

feed.title: DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) Price Target Raised to $153.00 at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has raised its price target for DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) to $153.00 from $150.00, maintaining a "buy" rating and implying a 12.20% upside. This adjustment comes amidst a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $147.62, following DTE's recent earnings beat and positive FY2025 and FY2026 EPS guidance. The company's stock currently trades around $136.37 with a P/E of approximately 20.5.

feed.title: Western Digital Stock Rises On Seagate's Strong Q2 Results
feed.summary: Western Digital (WDC) shares are trading higher after peer Seagate Technology Holdings (STX) reported strong Q2 results, surpassing analyst expectations for earnings and revenue. Seagate's success, driven by strong cloud and data-center demand fueled by AI, suggests positive industry tailwinds that could benefit Western Digital when it reports its own Q2 earnings on Thursday. Western Digital's stock was up over 12% following Seagate's upbeat report.

feed.title: Southern Company declares quarterly dividend of 74 cents per share
feed.summary: Southern Company (NYSE:SO) has announced a regular quarterly dividend of 74 cents per share, payable on March 6, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 17, 2026. The company has a strong dividend history, maintaining or increasing payments for 78 consecutive years. This announcement comes amid recent analyst downgrades and updates, and operational developments regarding power generation capacity expansion.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Roper Technologies (NASDAQ:ROP) Stock Price
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has downgraded Roper Technologies (NASDAQ:ROP) with a pessimistic forecast, cutting its price target from $457 to $397 and assigning an "underweight" rating. This follows Roper beating Q4 adjusted EPS but missing revenue expectations and providing Q1 and FY2026 guidance that was below Street estimates, leading to a stock selloff towards its 52-week low. Several other analysts have also trimmed their price targets for the industrial products company.

feed.title: Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) Stock Rating Upgraded by TD Securities
feed.summary: TD Securities has upgraded Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) to a "Buy" rating from "Hold," increasing its price target to C$209, suggesting an 11.08% upside. Despite this upgrade, the MarketBeat consensus remains a "Hold" with an average target of C$185.17. The article also details recent analyst reports and the bank's financial performance, including its Q4 EPS of C$3.28 and current trading price of C$188.15.

feed.title: JP Morgan Raises Price Target for Edison Intl (EIX) to $66 | EIX Stock News
feed.summary: JP Morgan has raised its price target for Edison International (EIX) to $66 from $65, maintaining a "Neutral" rating. This adjustment reflects a 1.54% increase and follows various analyst evaluations of EIX. The average target price from 14 analysts is $66.11, with an average brokerage recommendation of 2.5, indicating "Outperform" status.

feed.title: DTE Stock (DTE) Sees Target Price Increase by Jefferies | DTE St
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its target price for DTE Energy (DTE) from $150 to $153, maintaining a "Buy" rating, reflecting optimism for the stock. While other analysts like Mizuho have also raised targets, Wells Fargo and Barclays lowered theirs. Despite varied analyst opinions, the average target price from 15 analysts is $146.40, implying a 7.64% upside from its current price.

feed.title: CHD: Jefferies Raises Price Target and Maintains Buy Rating | CH
feed.summary: Jefferies has reaffirmed its 'Buy' rating for Church & Dwight Co (CHD) and increased the price target to $106.00, indicating a positive outlook. This adjustment comes amidst mixed reactions from other analysts, with some maintaining neutral or hold ratings and varied price target changes. The average target price from 19 analysts is $95.95, suggesting a 4.65% upside from the current price, while GuruFocus estimates a one-year GF Value of $109.97, implying a 19.94% upside.

feed.title: Tyler Technologies stock hits 52-week low at $421.26
feed.summary: Tyler Technologies (TYL) stock recently hit a 52-week low of $421.26, representing a 31.25% decline over the past year. Despite this downturn, the company maintains a "GOOD" financial health score and analysts like Goldman Sachs and Stifel have initiated coverage with "Buy" ratings, suggesting potential upside due to increased technology adoption in government and robust SaaS growth.

feed.title: 78 years without a cut: Southern Company keeps $0.74 dividend
feed.summary: Southern Company (NYSE:SO) has declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.74 per share, payable on March 6, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 17, 2026. This marks the 78th consecutive year the company has maintained a dividend equal to or greater than the previous quarter, demonstrating a long history of consistent shareholder returns. The utility provider serves 9 million customers across the Southeast and reiterated its commitment to continuous dividend payouts.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp says CEO Kedia will add board chair title
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp CEO Gunjan Kedia will add the role of board chair in April, taking over from Andy Cecere. This move returns the company to its previous governance structure where the CEO also holds the chair title, a common practice among large U.S. banks. While some investors advocate for splitting these roles, most major U.S. banks have consolidated them, often appointing a lead independent director to provide oversight.

feed.title: RBC Calls International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) a Defensive AI Play as Red Hat Headwinds Ease
feed.summary: RBC Capital has reiterated an Outperform rating for International Business Machines Corporation (IBM), calling it a "defensive AI play" with a $350.00 price target. The firm expects solid Q4 2025 earnings with potential upside in revenue and free cash flow, noting that anticipated easing of Red Hat pressure in fiscal 2026 will drive future growth. RBC highlights IBM's secular positioning in hybrid cloud and AI, and its long-term opportunity in quantum computing.

feed.title: F5: Quality Business, Uncomfortable Entry Point After Q1 Results (NASDAQ:FFIV)
feed.summary: F5, Inc. reported strong Q1 results with 7% revenue growth and raised its FY26 guidance, driven by systems revenue, AI customer wins, and regulatory tailwinds. Despite this positive performance and a significant stock surge, the author maintains a neutral hold rating due to the stock trading at a premium and persistent operational and competitive risks. High gross margins face pressure from rising memory costs, though healthy cash flow supports stock buybacks.

feed.title: Entrata Is Said to Work With Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan on IPO
feed.summary: Entrata, a property software company backed by Silver Lake and Blackstone Inc., is reportedly working with Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and JPMorgan Chase & Co. for an initial public offering. The company aims to complete the share sale as early as April, potentially raising around $500 million.

feed.title: What 26 Analyst Ratings Have To Say About Albemarle
feed.summary: Over the past three months, 26 analysts have rated Albemarle (NYSE: ALB), providing a diverse range of perspectives. The average price target has increased by 35.1% to $153.96, with a high of $230.00 and a low of $85.00. Despite this, the company has faced a revenue decline of 3.46% and shows net margin, ROE, and ROA below industry benchmarks, indicating challenges in profitability and asset utilization.

feed.title: CoStar pushes back on ‘short-sighted’ call to ditch Homes.com
feed.summary: CoStar Group has forcefully rejected activist investor Third Point's criticism of its Homes.com investment and overall strategy. The company defended Homes.com as core to its long-term vision, citing its expanding market reach and improved financials, while also pointing to board changes, buybacks, and a planned reduction in spending. CoStar questioned Third Point's track record and reaffirmed its commitment to its disciplined growth strategy.

feed.title: Fund Update: 392,870 QUALCOMM (QCOM) shares added to LORING WOLCOTT & COOLIDGE FIDUCIARY ADVISORS LLP|MA portfolio
feed.summary: LORING WOLCOTT & COOLIDGE FIDUCIARY ADVISORS LLP|MA has increased its holdings in QUALCOMM (QCOM) by 392,870 shares as of their latest SEC 13F filing for the 12-31-2025 report period. The article also details significant hedge fund activity, insider trading (predominantly sales), and congressional trades related to QCOM. Analyst ratings for QCOM are mostly positive, with a median price target of $190.0 from 10 analysts.

feed.title: Salesforce (CRM) Wins $5.6 Billion U.S. Army Technology Contract
feed.summary: Salesforce (CRM) has secured a significant 10-year, $5.6 billion Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract with the U.S. Army through its subsidiary Computable Insights LLC. This agreement aims to modernize the Army's operations by providing Salesforce's data fabric and cloud technologies to enhance decision-making, streamline operations, and improve support for military personnel. The contract continues Salesforce's decade-long relationship with the U.S. Armed Forces and will integrate fragmented data into a single platform for mission readiness.

feed.title: Wipro and Factory Partner to Accelerate Agent-Native Software Development For Enterprises Globally
feed.summary: Wipro and Factory have announced a partnership aimed at accelerating agent-native software development for enterprises worldwide. This collaboration seeks to enhance the capabilities of businesses to develop and deploy advanced software solutions using agent-native technologies. The initiative is expected to bring significant benefits to enterprises looking to innovate and streamline their software development processes.

feed.title: Raymond James Reiterates Outperform on Microsoft (MSFT) Ahead of Earnings
feed.summary: Raymond James analyst Andrew Marok reiterated an Outperform rating on Microsoft (MSFT) with a $600 price target ahead of the company's fiscal second-quarter 2026 earnings report. While the firm notes robust Azure performance, it remains cautious due to potential AI memory constraints. Raymond James suggests sentiment on MSFT stock has been declining, but overall, it is positive about the company's earnings outlook.

feed.title: Jersey Mike’s Said to Work With Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan on IPO
feed.summary: Jersey Mike's Subs is reportedly collaborating with Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan Chase & Co. for an initial public offering (IPO). The Blackstone Inc.-backed sandwich chain, which boasts over 3,000 locations, aims to complete its first-time share sale as early as the third quarter of this year.

feed.title: Microchip Technology Shares Rise After BofA Upgrade
feed.summary: Shares of Microchip Technology rose after BofA Securities upgraded the company's rating to "Buy" from "Neutral" and increased its price target to $95 from $78. This upgrade follows several recent positive analyst adjustments and news regarding product expansions and insider transactions. Microchip Technology specializes in integrated semiconductors for various sectors including household appliances, automotive, and telecommunications.

feed.title: Dow Earnings Preview: Can Cost Cuts Offset Deepening Industry Slump? By Investing.com
feed.summary: Dow Inc. is expected to report another quarterly loss, with analysts projecting a 46 cents per share loss on $9.46 billion in revenue due to a challenging chemical industry environment. The company's stock trades at its average analyst target, indicating a difficult quarter is already priced in. Investors are focused on Dow's cost-cutting measures, such as facility closures, and the impact of global overcapacity and subdued demand on its financial performance and liquidity.

feed.title: Jack Henry & Associates' (JKHY) "Buy" Rating Reiterated at DA Davidson
feed.summary: DA Davidson has reiterated a "Buy" rating for Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) with a $190 price target, suggesting a 4.8% upside. The company surpassed its quarterly earnings and revenue estimates and provided an optimistic FY2026 EPS guidance. Despite recent insider selling, institutional investors hold a significant majority of the stock.

feed.title: Peapack Private Appoints Elizabeth Martone as Senior Managing Director
feed.summary: Peapack-Gladstone Financial Corporation and Peapack Private Bank & Trust have appointed Elizabeth “Liz” Martone as Senior Managing Director, Group Director, operating from their New York City office. With 25 years of financial services experience, Martone will be a key part of James Handal’s leadership team, focusing on building client relationships, driving business development, and enhancing operational efficiency. She previously held senior roles at BankUnited, The Montauk Group, Herald National Bank, Capital One, and North Fork Bank.

feed.title: A Look At Annaly Capital Management (NLY) Valuation As Earnings And Short Interest Draw New Attention
feed.summary: Annaly Capital Management (NLY) is approaching its Q4 2025 earnings report with increased short interest and expectations for higher revenue. While Simply Wall St's narrative model suggests NLY is slightly overvalued at $24.36 (fair value $23.34), their discounted cash flow (DCF) model indicates a significant undervaluation, with an estimated future cash flow value of $42.82. The article highlights the conflicting valuations and encourages readers to explore the underlying assumptions and risks.

feed.title: Church & Dwight (NYSE:CHD) Price Target Raised to $106.00 at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has increased its price target for Church & Dwight (NYSE:CHD) to $106.00 from $102.00, reiterating a "buy" rating and suggesting a 15.71% upside. Other analysts have varying price targets and ratings, leading to a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $98.85 for the consumer products company. Church & Dwight recently surpassed Q3 earnings and revenue estimates and provided positive guidance for Q4 and FY2025.

feed.title: Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) Given New $230.00 Price Target at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has raised its price target for Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) to $230.00, up from $210.00, maintaining a "buy" rating. This new target implies a potential upside of 24.22% from the stock's previous close. Despite the positive analyst outlook, Albemarle shares traded down 4.6% to $185.16 during midday trading.

feed.title: Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) Price Target Raised to $195.00 at JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has raised its price target for Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) from $80.00 to $195.00, while maintaining a "neutral" rating, suggesting a potential upside of 5.31%. This adjustment comes amidst several other firms, including HSBC and Deutsche Bank, also upgrading Albemarle's rating and price targets. Despite these upgrades, Albemarle's stock recently traded down by 4.6% to $185.16, influenced by negative trailing EPS and mixed revenue results.

feed.title: PPG Industries (NYSE:PPG) Price Target Raised to $125.00 at Mizuho
feed.summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for PPG Industries (NYSE:PPG) to $125.00 from $118.00, maintaining an "outperform" rating and implying a potential upside of 9.57%. This adjustment follows PPG's Q4 earnings, where revenue of $3.91 billion exceeded estimates, but EPS of $1.51 missed consensus forecasts, and FY2026 EPS guidance was slightly below Street expectations. Despite the EPS miss and some negative sentiment regarding regional softness, the stock rose 3.4% on the news, and analysts generally have a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $120.86.

feed.title: Target's incoming CEO addresses 'Minneapolis violence' in memo to employees
feed.summary: In a recent memo to employees, Target's incoming CEO, Michael Fiddelke, addressed the "violence and loss of life" in Minneapolis following two fatal shootings by immigration agents and the detention of two Target workers. While Target has refrained from a public statement, Fiddelke assured employees of the company's focus on safety and support, amid criticism of Minnesota's large companies for their perceived silence on the immigration crackdown and Target's recent struggles, including boycotts and falling off Fortune's "Most Admired All-Stars" list.

feed.title: Refresh Your Closet with the Coca‑Cola® x adidas Collection
feed.summary: Coca-Cola and Adidas are collaborating on a new collection featuring several footwear styles like the Samba, Superstar, F50, Predator, and Adilette Slides, set to release in Summer 2026. The collection incorporates special graphics, branding elements, and ruby-colored accents. This partnership is timed with the upcoming FIFA World Cup, recalling their previous collaboration during the 2002 World Cup.

feed.title: Striking Parker Lord workers back at work after ratifying contract
feed.summary: Workers at Parker Lord in Summit Township have returned to work after ratifying a new contract. This agreement brings an end to the strike that had been ongoing.

feed.title: Oracle’s Data Center Attracts Potential Investment From Blackstone: Report
feed.summary: Blackstone is reportedly considering a debt investment in Oracle's Michigan data center project, which is part of the larger Stargate Project aimed at bolstering AI infrastructure. This comes amid declining Oracle shares and rising debt insurance costs, raising questions among some investors about the project's viability. Despite these concerns, representatives from Oracle, Bank of America, and Related Digital affirm that financing is progressing as planned for the project, which has faced regulatory and public scrutiny.

feed.title: PROG Holdings, Inc. to Release Fourth Quarter 2025 Financial Results on February 18, 2026
feed.summary: PROG Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: PRG) announced it will release its fourth quarter 2025 financial results on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, before the market opens. The fintech holding company will also host a live webcast at 8:30 A.M. ET on the same day to discuss these results. Interested parties can access the webcast through the company's investor relations website.

feed.title: PROG Holdings, Inc. to Release Fourth Quarter 2025 Financial Results on February 18, 2026
feed.summary: PROG Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:PRG) announced it will release its fourth-quarter 2025 financial results on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, before market open. The fintech holding company will also host a live webcast at 8:30 AM ET on the same day to discuss these results. Interested parties can access the webcast via the investor relations section of the PROG Holdings website.

feed.title: Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce becomes big Sleep Number investor
feed.summary: Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce has become an investor and strategic partner in Sleep Number, signing a three-year deal. This partnership makes him one of the company's top shareholders and aims to help improve its financial performance. Kelce will also serve as a spokesman for the smart bed company.

feed.title: Why has TikTok been glitching? Oracle says data center outage led to issues plaguing U.S. users
feed.summary: TikTok users in the U.S. experienced glitches and posting issues, leading to claims of censorship, particularly against anti-Trump and anti-ICE content. However, Oracle Corp., one of TikTok's new U.S. owners, stated that the technical problems were due to a weather-related power outage at a data center. This outage occurred shortly after TikTok's Chinese owner, ByteDance, finalized a deal to establish a U.S.-majority-owned joint venture to prevent a ban.

feed.title: Is New Gold (NGD) a Solid Growth Stock? 3 Reasons to Think "Yes"
feed.summary: New Gold (NGD) has been identified as a strong growth stock by Zacks' proprietary system, earning a Growth Score of A and a top Zacks Rank. Key reasons supporting this include projected EPS growth of 112.3% this year, a year-over-year cash flow growth of 41.6%, and promising upward revisions in current-year earnings estimates. These factors combine to position New Gold for potential outperformance in the market.

feed.title: Here's Why You Should Retain Ecolab Stock in Your Portfolio Now
feed.summary: Ecolab (ECL) is recommended for retention in portfolios due to its strong product portfolio, robust third-quarter 2025 performance, and continuous focus on research and development, particularly in its Global High-Tech business and digital platform. Despite potential headwinds from macroeconomic factors, the company's diversified offerings in water treatment, hygiene, and life sciences, coupled with strategic R&D investments and strong growth engines, suggest a positive outlook. Ecolab has consistently surpassed Zacks Consensus Estimates for earnings, delivering an average surprise of 0.27% over the past four quarters.

feed.title: General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) Shares Gap Down - What's Next?
feed.summary: General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) shares gapped down following its latest earnings report, despite beating Q4 estimates with EPS of $4.17 and revenue of $14.38 billion. The company reported a rising backlog of $118 billion, bolstered by a significant U.S. Navy contract win. However, concerns remain regarding a $41 million tariff hit, increased capital expenditure, and supply-chain cost pressures that could affect free cash flow and margins in the near term, although analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating.

feed.title: NextNav (NASDAQ:NN) Trading Up 5.5% - Here's Why
feed.summary: NextNav (NASDAQ:NN) shares rose 5.5% on Wednesday, trading at $15.1190. Despite the price increase, trading volume was down 63%, and the company remains unprofitable with a market capitalization of $2.04 billion. Analyst ratings are mixed, with some upgrades to 'hold' but a MarketBeat consensus of 'Reduce', while insider activity shows both significant purchases and sales.

feed.title: Sigma Lithium (NASDAQ:SGML) Trading Down 7.2% - Here's Why
feed.summary: Sigma Lithium (NASDAQ:SGML) experienced a 7.2% decline in its stock price, trading down to $12.59 intraday on Wednesday with significantly lower trading volume. This drop comes amidst weak analyst sentiment, with a consensus "Reduce" rating and an average target price of $17.17, following downgraded ratings from firms like Citigroup and Bank of America. The company's financial fundamentals remain challenged, highlighted by a large revenue miss in its last quarterly earnings, negative net margin and ROE, and weak liquidity.

feed.title: Oric Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ORIC) Shares Down 6.8% - Time to Sell?
feed.summary: Oric Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ORIC) saw its shares fall 6.8% to $11.2850, with trading volume significantly below average. Despite a largely positive analyst sentiment with a "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $19.90, some insiders, including CEO Jacob Chacko, have recently sold shares. The company, focused on developing small molecule therapeutics for solid tumor oncology, has a market cap of $1.11 billion and reported better-than-expected EPS in its last quarterly earnings.

feed.title: What Is Going On With Texas Instruments Stock On Wednesday? - Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN)
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (NASDAQ: TXN) stock rose after analysts increased price targets following a strong quarter. Despite slightly missing Q4 revenue and EPS forecasts, the company's outlook signaled steadying demand, with improved order patterns, growing backlog, and strong performance in the industrial and data center segments. Analysts from Cantor Fitzgerald, Benchmark, and Rosenblatt all reiterated positive ratings and raised price targets, highlighting an anticipated broader chip-cycle recovery.

feed.title: Honeywell collaborates with SAF One and Tata Projects to enable SAF production
feed.summary: Honeywell has announced a collaboration with SAF One and Tata Projects to facilitate the production of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF). This partnership aims to advance the availability and adoption of SAF, contributing to reduced carbon emissions in the aviation industry. The initiative highlights a joint effort to develop and implement solutions for sustainable fuel production.
